column1: LINKEDIN — Username OSINT Data Point Map
Tab 3.5 of Username_OSINT_DataPoints.xlsx — Professional network OSINT for identity confirmation

column3: INVESTIGATIVE VALUE
Unique identifier — custom URL reveals real name or chosen handle
Real name — LinkedIn enforces real-name policy; high identity confidence
Professional photo — reverse image search for other accounts, face match
Current role and company — reveals employer, industry, seniority
Self-written bio — often copied to other platforms; NLP similarity match
Employer verification — cross-check against company records, MCA filings
Career trail — confirms identity timeline, detects false claims, links to colleagues
College/University — confirms identity, links to alumni networks
Expertise mapping — identifies technical capabilities relevant to investigation
Network size indicator — <500 may indicate fake/new account
Social graph overlap — shared connections confirm real-world association
Geographic presence — current city + country for jurisdiction mapping
Direct identifiers — visible to connections; email links to other accounts
External URL — leads to personal site, GitHub, other social handles
Behaviour & opinion trail — writing style, topics, associates who engage
Communication network — who subject interacts with, communication style
Professional credentials — confirms claimed qualifications, links to certifying bodies
Values and affiliations — NGOs, political causes, religious organisations
Associate network — recommenders are confirmed real-world contacts
Who subject vouches for — reveals close professional relationships
Interest mapping — sectors, competitors, topics subject monitors
Community affiliations — industry groups, alumni groups, niche communities
Account age — new account during incident = suspicious; old = credible
Same photo across platforms — pHash match even if cropped/resized
Current employment status — useful for social engineering context, timing
Multilingual profile — narrows regional identity, corroborates location data
Full associate network — who subject is directly connected to
Private communications — direct evidence of planning, coordination
Account-linked email — ties LinkedIn to other platform registrations
Access metadata — geolocation of logins, device fingerprint
